steps.title: Contrast hypothesis and result
steps.content: Researchers wondered whether the spiders spread because they were aggressive. The next sentence reports the opposite finding: the spiders are gentle and freeze when disturbed.

steps.title: Mark the reversal
steps.content: Choice A signals a result, but the discovery does not follow from the aggression hypothesis. Choice C signals an example, and Choice D signals a restatement. The transition must show that the finding replaces the expected explanation. The answer is B.
